module.name: output
module.spec: Drift supports exactly three output modes — Plain, Color, JSON. Plain is the byte-identical continuation of pre-output-layer output (the safe baseline). Color wraps semantic categories of output in ANSI sequences. JSON serializes per-command structured objects. Every command supports all three modes via global flags. See audiences.

The default mode is Color when (stdout is a TTY) AND (NO_COLOR env var is unset). In all other cases the default mode is Plain. The user can force Color with --color=always (overrides non-TTY and NO_COLOR) or force Plain with --no-color / --color=never. See TTY detection and flag precedence.

TTY detection uses os.Stdout.Stat() and checks (mode & os.ModeCharDevice) != 0. This is the stdlib-only test; golang.org/x/term is NOT used (preserves zero dependency). NO_COLOR is honored per https://no-color.org/: any non-empty value disables color when set, unless explicitly overridden by --color=always. See flag precedence.

Three global flags are accepted on EVERY subcommand (including help, skill, version): --json, --no-color, --color={auto,always,never}. These are stripped from args by a global pre-pass BEFORE per-subcommand flag validation runs. They never appear in any command's Meta().Flags list. They never trigger unknown_flag_rejection. See unknown flag rejection for the updated rule.

The global flag pre-pass scans args[1:] for the three global flags and removes them from the slice that the dispatched command sees. The pre-pass does NOT validate combinations (e.g. --json + --no-color is allowed; precedence is resolved later by SelectPresenter). The pre-pass handles both --color=VALUE and standalone --no-color / --json forms.

When multiple output mode indicators are present, the precedence (highest to lowest) is: (1) --json → JSON mode (ANSI never emitted, --no-color ignored); (2) --no-color → Plain mode; (3) --color=never → Plain; (4) --color=always → Color (overrides non-TTY and NO_COLOR); (5) NO_COLOR env var set → Plain; (6) stdout is not a TTY → Plain; (7) default → Color.

Plain mode is the byte-identical continuation of pre-output-layer output. PlainPresenter migrates the existing format* functions (formatTodo, formatList, formatShow*, formatDiff*, unlinkedMarkerWarning) verbatim. Plain output NEVER emits ANSI sequences. Plain output is the baseline for the guardrail property. See PlainPresenter.

Color mode is an independent presenter implementation that renders from Result data using shared prose constants. Color is NOT a wrapper over Plain; it formats each method's output from the Result independently. Color emits ANSI sequences per the palette. See color palette and ColorPresenter.

Color categories and their ANSI codes: diff + lines = green (32); diff - lines = red (31); hunk @@ headers = cyan (36); [DRIFTED], [deleted], error messages = red (31); [TODO], [unlinked], drift status lines = yellow (33); [synced], Status: in sync, No changes detected = green (32); section headers (=== Spec/Marker ===) = bold (1); → Run 'drift ...' hints = cyan (36). Bold = \x1b[1m; colors use \x1b[Nm; reset = \x1b[0m.

For every Result r and every Presenter method X, stripANSI(ColorPresenter.X(r)) MUST equal PlainPresenter.X(r) byte-for-byte. This is the correctness invariant that makes Color safe to implement independently of Plain. It is enforced by a battery test in cli/output/color_test.go covering: synced, drifted marker, drifted spec, both-changed, deleted spec, deleted marker, no-baseline, multi-edge expanded, --all with multiple edges, empty state, unlinked warning, and every Ok/Error/Text/Version shape. See guardrail test.

JSON mode serializes a per-command structured object via encoding/json. Output is a single JSON object with no trailing newline (main.go adds the newline). Field order is deterministic (struct-defined, not map[string]interface{}). JSON output NEVER contains ANSI sequences. See JSONPresenter and JSON schema.

Per-command JSON shapes: todo → {ok, specs, markers, links, todos:[{marker,spec,markerLocation,specLocation,markerChanged,specChanged,markerDeleted,specDeleted}], unlinkedMarkers}; list → {specs:[{id,filepath,deleted,unlinked,text?}], markers:[{id,filepath,startLine,endLine,deleted,unlinked,preview?}], links:[{marker,spec,status}]}; show → {kind,id,filepath,hash,lines?,content,linked:[...]}; diff edge → {spec:DiffSide,marker:DiffSide}; diff expanded/--all → {id?,edges:[...]}; init/link/unlink/reset → {ok,command,message}; help/skill → {text}; version → {version}; errors → {ok:false,error,hint?,exit}. Each DiffSide has {id,filepath?,lines?,baselineHash,currentHash,hasBaseline,deleted,baseline?,current?,patch?}.

RunWithRender returns a (Result, int) tuple where Result is a sealed interface implemented by exactly: TodoResult, ListResult, ShowResult, DiffEdgeResult, DiffExpandedResult, DiffAllResult, OkResult, ErrorResult, TextResult, VersionResult. Each Result type is a thin wrapper around orchestrator/core data. See result types impl.

Every error path produces an ErrorResult. There are no bare return err.Error() sites in the command pipeline. ErrorResult carries Command, Message, Hint, and Exit. Plain reproduces today's error text byte-identically; JSON emits {ok:false,error,hint?,exit}. See error uniformity.

RunWithRender executes the following ordered steps for every invocation: (1) stripGlobalFlags; (2) help_flag check; (3) unknown_flag_rejection check; (4) registry lookup; (5) construct orchestrator + CommandContext; (6) cmd.Run(ctx) → (Result, exitCode); (7) presenter.Render(result) → output string; (8) return (string, int). No step may be skipped for any command. See dispatcher.

Every command is a struct implementing the Command interface: Run(ctx CommandContext) (Result, int) and Meta() CommandMeta. The Run signature forces Result return (Go type system enforcement). See command interface impl.

Before any subcommand dispatches, three uniform checks run: (1) global_flag_stripping removes --json/--no-color/--color= from args; (2) help_flag intercepts cmd --help/-h and returns the command's usage text; (3) unknown_flag_rejection rejects any unrecognized --flag or -x. These checks run for EVERY subcommand including those that take no arguments. See unknown flag rejection.

The version command flows through the dispatch pipeline like every other command (was previously a main.go pre-check). Returns VersionResult{Version}. Plain prints "drift version <X>"; JSON prints {"version":"<X>"}. --version and -v at args[0] are normalized to "version" by main.go before dispatch.
